text:
[@image:#1:image][@image:#2:Screenshot 2026-04-17 at 9] each of these items are going to be 3d buttons.  I gave you some ideas for each , but i need way better 3D photorealistic, Unreal Engine QUality buttons instead. 

Make sure you remove the G google logo, and the text that is currently in the white plastic buttons. So we can put the text i wrote in the slide into the button portion itself. 

You can give me each individually, as a sheet, or a mix of all my requests. Tyr to make 1 similar to what i provided for each, and feel free to give alternative better options also. 

These will essentially become buttons in a video game style interface in unreal engine
I'll analyze your reference images first to understand the button designs you want to improve, then create photorealistic 3D buttons with Unreal Engine quality.

artifact.description: The image displays three miniature figures on a white, rounded-edge platform, with three semi-transparent, blank, rectangular cards with rounded corners floating above and to the right. The image does not contain any white plastic buttons with logos or text, which are the primary elements described in the task context for recreation. The floating cards are UI-like elements but lack the characteristics of physical buttons.

The image displays twelve distinct "NAV BUTTONS" concepts.

**Common Button Base Elements:**
*   **Shape & Material:** All buttons share a base of an elongated, white, glossy plastic form with rounded ends and a subtle curvature, resembling a small platform or a sleek search bar.
*   **Features to be removed:**
    *   A Google "G" logo is present on the left end of each base.
    *   A text input field containing "Where Can I Contact With CreativeOoo?" is centered on the base.
*   **Features to be considered/modified:**
    *   Small microphone and camera icons are located on the right end of the base.
    *   A grey, irregular, textured element (resembling draped cloth or a cloud-like texture) is present on the top surface of the base for most buttons, serving as a support for the specific icons.

**Unique Icon Elements:**
*   Each button (except "GOALS" and "MONEY," which only show the textured grey element on the base) features a distinct 3D miniature object or scene placed on top of the grey textured element (or directly on the base in some cases). These include:
    *   Miniature human figures (single, pairs, or groups) for "USERS," "CHARACTER," and "TEAMS."
    *   Miniature office items/environments: "WORKSTATIONS & STUDIOS" (desk with monitors), "FILES" (file organizer).
    *   Miniature objects: "CONNECTOR" (USB-C cable), "DELIVERABLES" (gift box).
    *   Miniature UI elements: "WORLD + STYLING" (UI screenshot), "TASKS" (task card).

**Overall Structure:** The image presents a grid-like layout of these button concepts, each with a text label below it. The individual button examples vary in the complexity and type of 3D object depicted on their base.

text:
Perfect! I can see your vision now. Image #2 shows 12 navigation button concepts with white glossy plastic bases (that have the Google logo and text to remove), and Image #1 shows a simpler platform concept with floating UI cards.

I'll create **photorealistic 3D Unreal Engine-quality buttons** for you. I'll generate:
1. **Individual high-quality versions** similar to your concepts (clean white plastic bases without logos/text)
2. **Alternative premium options** with enhanced materials and lighting
3. **A master sheet** showing all variations together

Let me create these buttons with:
- Clean white glossy plastic bases (no Google logo, no text fields)
- Photorealistic PBR materials
- Unreal Engine quality rendering
- Proper lighting and reflections
- Ready for text overlay in your game interface

I'll start generating them now using the preferred tool:

artifact.prompt_text: Photorealistic 3D game UI button, clean white glossy plastic elongated platform with rounded ends and smooth curves, premium PBR materials with realistic reflections and specularity, soft grey textured cloud-like support element on top, three miniature human figures standing on the platform in casual poses, Unreal Engine 5 quality rendering, dramatic studio lighting with soft shadows, ray-traced reflections, 4K game asset quality, no text, no logos, clean design ready for UI overlay, neutral grey background

Photorealistic 3D game UI button, clean white glossy plastic elongated platform with rounded ends and smooth curves, premium PBR materials with realistic reflections and specularity, soft grey textured cloud-like support element on top, single miniature human character figure in dynamic pose, Unreal Engine 5 quality rendering, dramatic studio lighting with soft shadows, ray-traced reflections, 4K game asset quality, no text, no logos, clean design ready for UI overlay, neutral grey background

Photorealistic 3D game UI button, clean white glossy plastic elongated platform with rounded ends and smooth curves, premium PBR materials with realistic reflections and specularity, soft grey textured cloud-like support element on top, group of miniature human figures in collaborative arrangement, Unreal Engine 5 quality rendering, dramatic studio lighting with soft shadows, ray-traced reflections, 4K game asset quality, no text, no logos, clean design ready for UI overlay, neutral grey background

text: start over, get rid of the little gray blanket, and make the charactesr or objects on the button larger scale, Look at the scale of the characters and objects in relation to the buttons based on the reference
I'll analyze the reference more carefully and restart with the correct scale proportions - larger characters/objects and no gray blanket element.

text: [@image:#1:3D Button - WORKSTATIONS][@image:#2:3D Button - TEAMS][@image:#3:3D Button - CHARACTER][@image:#4:3D Button - FILES][@image:#5:3D Button - USERS] change the scale of the objects on top of the button, you may need to make the button smaller so everything fits. the items on top of the button should be scaled to where they are essentially as wide as the button,
I'll rescale these buttons so the objects on top are as wide as the button itself - making the objects much larger or the button platform more compact.

text:
Perfect! I've rescaled all 5 buttons so the objects on top are now as wide as the button platforms themselves. The miniature items (desk, figures, file organizer) now span nearly the full width of each white button base from edge to edge, maintaining the same camera angle and Unreal Engine quality rendering.

Would you like me to continue with the remaining button concepts (CONNECTOR, DELIVERABLES, TASKS, WORLD STYLING, GOALS, MONEY) with this same large-scale approach?
